primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with concurrent AKI and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and low hemoglobin (9.8 g/dL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA score increase. Progressive AKI (Stage 1 to Stage 3) is evident with creatinine elevation (excluded outlier indicates rapid rise). Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Sepsis-3: suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation with rapid progression (excluded outlier shows 6.5 mg/dL rise in 6 hours) meets KDIGO Stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score 3 (platelets <50 K/uL indicates severe coagulopathy)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Platelets <150 K/uL initiates SOFA coagulation scoring

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Requires vasopressor-dependent hypotension plus lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis bundle per Sepsis-3 protocol including broad-spectrum antibiotics
Order blood cultures and infection source investigation
Begin fluid resuscitation and monitor response
Assess need for vasopressors if hypotension develops
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
doctor_handoff: Review for definitive infection source, assess volume status, and evaluate for ICU admission. Prioritize renal replacement therapy consultation given AKI Stage 3.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 - excluded outlier) with platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
